title: Coffee on Cotton
timeEstimate: 10–30 minutes (fresh); aged adds a peroxide cycle
stainChemistry: Coffee is a tannin stain — polyphenols and browning pigments (brown roast compounds) from coffee beans. Fresh coffee is water-soluble and lifts easily; aged coffee oxidizes to a deeper brown and lock together weakly to cellulose. This card is the general coffee case — for black coffee specifically see coffee-black-cotton, for coffee with cream see coffee-cream-cotton (different chemistry with added protein + fat). If the coffee has ANY cream or milk in it, treat as coffee-with-cream — the protein ordering rule changes everything.
whyThisWorks: Cotton handles the classic tannin sequence cleanly: cold flush + NSD for fresh, acid-side tannin spotter + 3% H₂O₂ for aged. Hot wash finishes. The one rule that saves most coffee stains is avoiding alkaline products (ammonia, baking soda, soap scum) — those permanently darken tannin.
defaultAssumption: Coffee on cotton is a high-confidence DIY in almost every case. Fresh = trivial; aged = one extra cycle. Escalation rarely needed unless heat-set.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold water, white cotton terry towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cold flush from the BACK of the fabric. Blot from underneath. Most fresh coffee lifts here.
Apply NSD, sponge gently. Blot with a clean towel.
Under 30 min old only. Hot pour-through from the back. Skip on aged stains.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A) — acid-side tannin treatment
spottingProtocol.instruction: Acid-side tannin spotter by dropper on residual brown. Dwell 2–5 min, blot. Repeat once if needed.
3% H₂O₂ on residual color. Dwell 3–5 min, blot. One oxidizer at a time.
Rinse until clean. Hot wash per care label.

steps: 4. Acid-side tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter / PRENETT-A). 2–5 min.
6. NSD flush, hot wash per care label.
products: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold through spotting; hot wash post-chemistry
warnings: Never use hot water on aged coffee — sets tannin
If coffee has cream / milk, switch to the protein-first coffee-cream-cotton protocol

homeSolutions.instruction: Fresh coffee on cotton is a simple stain. Hold the BACK of the stained area under cold running water for 30–60 seconds. Cold only — hot water sets coffee tannin brown. Most of the stain lifts in this step alone.

If the coffee had milk or cream in it, this isn't just a tannin stain — there's also protein that needs to come off before heat touches it. Use the coffee-cream-cotton protocol instead. The difference: that protocol starts with an enzyme pretreat, because protein-before-heat is non-negotiable. Black coffee: continue below.
A drop of Dawn dish soap worked into the spot with a fingertip for a minute. Rinse cool. Clears residual pigment.

homeSolutions.agent: White Vinegar + Water for Stubborn Brown
homeSolutions.instruction: Mix 1 teaspoon white vinegar with 3 tablespoons cool water. Dab on the spot, let sit 3 minutes, blot. Breaks the tannin bond.
For residual brown shadow on white cotton: dab 3% drugstore hydrogen peroxide, 3–5 min, blot. Skip on dark/dyed cotton.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia.

homeSolutions.agent: Hot Wash, Inspect Before the Dryer
homeSolutions.instruction: Launder hot per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer — residual tannin sets permanently under heat.

If coffee has been through a hot dryer or two home rounds didn't clear it, take to a dry cleaner. Tell them: "Coffee on cotton — [black / with cream], age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + vinegar + 3% peroxide."

steps: 1. Coffee WITH CREAM? Use coffee-cream-cotton — different chemistry.
2. Cold water flush from the BACK.
4. Diluted white vinegar for residual brown.
6. Hot wash per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if coffee has been hot-dried and home round didn't clear it.
warnings: Coffee WITH cream is a different stain — use coffee-cream-cotton protocol
Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia

neverDo: Never use hot water on aged coffee — sets tannin
Never treat coffee-with-cream with this protocol — protein needs enzyme first
fiberSensitivities: Cotton tolerates NSD, acid-side spotters, 3% H₂O₂, hot wash
Dyed cotton: skip or inside-seam-test peroxide

materialWarnings: Coffee with cream = different protocol (protein first)
Hot water only for fresh — sets aged tannin
Peroxide on whites; test inside seam on dark cotton

escalation.when: Coffee heat-set by iron or dryer, or two home rounds plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Coffee on cotton — [black / with cream], age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + vinegar + 3% H₂O₂."

contextQuestions: Black coffee or with cream/milk? (cream version is a different protocol)
Is the cotton white, light, or dark/dyed?
Has it been through a hot wash or dryer?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Black coffee is simple tannin. Cream version is combination-stain chemistry (protein first). The biggest home-treatment error on coffee is treating cream-coffee as plain coffee and hot-washing it before the enzyme step.
